name: Shrimp with Lobster Sauce #1
recipeInstructions:
Contrary to popular belief, lobster sauce does not contain lobster. It's delicious served OVER lobster or shrimp.
Lobster Sauce: Soak salted black beans for 10 minutes; rinse and drain. Mash with garlic. Cut green onions into half-inch sections. In a small bowl, beat egg with water.
Shrimp: Shell, devein, and butterfly the shrimp. To butterfly the shrimp, slice along the back about three-quarters of the way through the flesh with a small paring knife. Spread the shrimp open; cover but do not refrigerate.
To stir-fry, heat the wok to medium-high. When hot, dribble oil around the side of the pan. Put the shrimp in the wok all at once, and start tossing. Keep the shrimp in motion so they will cook uniformly. When they just begin to curl, remove them to the serving dish.
Heat the wok to medium; add the remaining oil. When the oil is hot (not smoking), add garlic/bean mixture. Stir briskly until the aroma of beans and garlic is strong, but do not overcook. Add ground pork in small pieces; stir-fry until cooked, about 4 minutes. Add broth, soy sauce, and sugar, stirring while the mixture comes to a boil. Thicken the mixture with cornstarch paste to create a light cream-like sauce (not too thick). Slowly stir in beaten egg. Cook briefly. Add shrimp. Mix and serve.
